vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1441

plan.title: Windows installer binary auto-triggers UAC elevation (install-* filename heuristic + no asInvoker manifest); breaks headless --yes installs/upgrades

narratives.Description: Windows installer binary auto-triggers UAC elevation (install-* filename heuristic + no asInvoker manifest); breaks headless --yes installs/upgrades
narratives.Problem: The released Windows installer binaries (install-windows-amd64.exe / -arm64.exe) auto-trigger a UAC elevation prompt on launch despite needing no admin rights, because (1) the install-* filename matches Windows' legacy installer-detection heuristic and (2) the Go binary ships with no application manifest declaring an execution level. This breaks the canonical headless flow (deft-install --yes --upgrade --repo-root . --json) on Windows: the prompt blocks unattended runs or fails with 'requires elevation'.
narratives.Action: Embed a Windows application manifest declaring requestedExecutionLevel level=asInvoker uiAccess=false via per-arch .syso resources committed in cmd/deft-install/ (named resource_windows_amd64.syso / resource_windows_arm64.syso so go build links them only for GOOS=windows), generated with goversioninfo (or rsrc). Wire generation into scripts/build_dist.py / the release path and keep Linux cross-compilation working. Add a build/release check verifying the embedded manifest declares asInvoker. The optional asset rename is NOT pursued; the manifest fix is filename-robust.
narratives.Test: A check verifies the embedded manifest declares level=asInvoker (parse the committed manifest/versioninfo or the built PE resource). Confirm linux/darwin/windows cross-builds still succeed and the windows .syso links only on windows. Acceptance (manual): install-windows-amd64.exe from a non-elevated shell raises no UAC prompt and --yes --upgrade completes unattended.
narratives.Overview:
## Severity

**High on Windows / affects all Windows users.** The released Windows installer binary auto-triggers a **UAC elevation prompt** on launch even though it needs no admin rights. Interactively it's a surprising prompt; **non-interactively it breaks the canonical headless flow** — `deft-install --yes --upgrade --repo-root . --json` cannot run unattended (agent/CI) because the elevation prompt blocks or the process fails with an elevation error. This defeats the "have your agent do the upgrade" story on Windows (related: #1322).

## Symptoms (observed this session)

Running the official `install-windows-amd64.exe` (v0.39.x) on Windows 11 + pwsh 7:
- Interactive launch raised a UAC prompt; declining it returned:
  ```
  Program 'deft-install.exe' failed to run: An error occurred trying to start process ... The operation was canceled by the user.
  ```
- Non-interactive `Start-Process -NoNewWindow` failed with:
  ```
  Start-Process: This command cannot be run due to the error: The requested operation requires elevation.
  ```
- An earlier plain invocation appeared to "succeed" (exit 0) but did nothing — consistent with the elevated child never running.

**What unblocked it:** setting `__COMPAT_LAYER=RunAsInvoker` (bypasses Windows installer-detection and runs as the invoking user) **and/or** renaming the binary to a name without `install` (e.g. `dftbin.exe`). With both, the binary ran with no prompt and completed `--yes --upgrade` successfully (exit 0).

## Root cause

Two compounding factors:
1. **Filename heuristic.** Windows' legacy *installer detection* auto-elevates executables whose names contain tokens like `install`, `setup`, `update`, `patch`. The asset is named `install-windows-amd64.exe` / `install-windows-arm64.exe`, so it matches.
2. **No embedded `asInvoker` manifest.** Installer detection is applied **only** to executables that lack an application manifest declaring an execution level. The Go binary ships without one, so the heuristic kicks in. A manifest declaring `asInvoker` disables the heuristic regardless of filename.

## Proposed fix

**Primary — embed a Windows application manifest with `asInvoker`** in the Windows builds:
```xml
<requestedExecutionLevel level="asInvoker" uiAccess="false" />
```
Generate a `.syso` resource carrying this manifest and place it in `cmd/deft-install/` so `go build` for `GOOS=windows` links it automatically (per-arch `.syso` for amd64 + arm64). Tooling options: `github.com/josephspurrier/goversioninfo` or `akavel/rsrc`. Wire it into `scripts/build_dist.py` / the release workflow. This makes UAC stay silent even though the file is named `install-*.exe`, and restores headless capability.

**Secondary (optional, not a substitute)** — rename the release assets to drop the heuristic token (e.g. `deft-windows-amd64.exe`). Note this is a breaking change for the README + #1411/#1413 banner download links, and is fragile (a user who renames it back re-triggers the heuristic). The manifest fix is robust to any filename and should land regardless.

**Interim doc/workaround** — until the manifest ships, document the `__COMPAT_LAYER=RunAsInvoker` workaround for Windows headless/agent runs.

## Acceptance criteria

- The released Windows binaries (amd64 + arm64), run from a non-elevated shell, do **not** raise a UAC prompt and do **not** fail with "requires elevation" — even named `install-*.exe`.
- `deft-install --yes --upgrade --repo-root . --json` completes unattended on Windows (no prompt, no elevation error).
- A build/release check verifies the embedded manifest declares `level="asInvoker"`.

## Related

- #1322 — installer agent-mode / non-interactive hardening (UAC elevation is a Windows-specific reason headless runs fail).
- #1411 / #1413 — upgrade banner + release-notes guidance pointing Windows users at the canonical headless command.

Evidence: reproduced with `deft-install v0.39.2`–`v0.39.4` on Windows 11 + PowerShell 7.6.2; workaround `__COMPAT_LAYER=RunAsInvoker` + neutral filename consistently bypassed the prompt.

items.title: Embed an asInvoker Windows application manifest via per-arch .syso resources (resource_windows_amd64.syso / resource_windows_arm64.syso) in cmd/deft-install/, generated with goversioninfo/rsrc
Wire .syso generation into scripts/build_dist.py / the release path; keep linux/darwin/windows cross-builds green and windows-only linkage intact
Add a build/release check that verifies the embedded manifest declares level=asInvoker
Released Windows binaries (amd64+arm64) run from a non-elevated shell with no UAC prompt; --yes --upgrade --repo-root . --json completes unattended

references.title: Issue #1441: Windows installer binary auto-triggers UAC elevation (install-* filename heuristic + no asInvoker manifest); breaks headless --yes installs/upgrades
